```{r frag-path-scatter}
    geom_point(data = tank_avg, aes(x = xm, y = ym, fill = TREATMENT),
               shape = 21, size = 2.8, color = "white", stroke = 0.7, alpha = 0.95) +
    annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = label,
             hjust = -0.06, vjust = 1.5, size = 2.6,
             color = col, fontface = "italic") +
    scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, guide = "none") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
    labs(x = xl, y = yl, title = title) +
    path_pt
}

leg_plot_diag <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(pH_tank, log_chla, fill = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, size = 3, color = "white", stroke = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.text     = element_text(size = 8.5),
        legend.key.size = unit(0.45, "cm"))

pp1 <- mk_path("pH_tank",    "log_chla",    "Mean pH",                             "log(Chl-a, \u00b5g cm\u207b\u00b2)",                             "log_chla",    "pH_tank",    "pH \u2192 Chl-a",                  TRUE)
pp2 <- mk_path("DOC_tank",   "log_chla",    "DOC (\u00b5mol C L\u207b\u00b9)",      "log(Chl-a, \u00b5g cm\u207b\u00b2)",                             "log_chla",    "DOC_tank",   "DOC \u2192 Chl-a",                 FALSE)
pp3 <- mk_path("log_chla",   "log_endo",    "log(Chl-a, \u00b5g cm\u207b\u00b2)",   "log(Endosymbionts cm\u207b\u00b2)",                              "log_endo",    "log_chla",   "Chl-a \u2192 Endo density",        TRUE)
pp4 <- mk_path("log_endo",   "log_cpe",     "log(Endosymbionts cm\u207b\u00b2)",    "log(Chl-a per cell)",                                            "log(cpe)",    "log_endo",   "Endo density \u2192 Chl-a / cell", TRUE)
pp5 <- mk_path("log_endo",   "log_biomass", "log(Endosymbionts cm\u207b\u00b2)",    "log(Biomass, mg cm\u207b\u00b2)",                                "log_biomass", "log_endo",   "Endo density \u2192 Biomass",      TRUE)
pp6 <- mk_path("log_endo",   "mean_GP",     "log(Endosymbionts cm\u207b\u00b2)",    "GP (\u00b5mol O\u2082 cm\u207b\u00b2 hr\u207b\u00b9)",           "mean_GP",     "log_endo",   "Endo density \u2192 GP",           TRUE)
pp7 <- mk_path("mean_GP",    "mean_R",      "GP (\u00b5mol O\u2082 cm\u207b\u00b2 hr\u207b\u00b9)", "R (\u00b5mol O\u2082 cm\u207b\u00b2 hr\u207b\u00b9)", "mean_R",  "mean_GP",    "GP \u2192 Respiration",             TRUE)
pp8 <- mk_path("log_biomass","mean_R",      "log(Biomass, mg cm\u207b\u00b2)",      "R (\u00b5mol O\u2082 cm\u207b\u00b2 hr\u207b\u00b9)",           "mean_R",      "log_biomass","Biomass \u2192 Respiration",        FALSE)

(pp1 | pp2 | pp3 | pp4) /
(pp5 | pp6 | pp7 | pp8) /
(cowplot::get_legend(leg_plot_diag) |> wrap_elements()) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1, 0.10))
```

### Path interpretations

**pH → Chl-a (β\* = 0.257, p = 0.017)**
Daytime pH is elevated by photosynthetic activity — both by the corals' own symbionts and by the surrounding benthic community. A positive path from pH to chlorophyll density means that corals in more metabolically productive, higher-pH environments maintain greater pigment investment per unit area. Mechanistically, favorable light and carbon conditions (reflected in elevated pH) signal symbionts to upregulate chlorophyll synthesis; this is consistent with photoacclimation theory where pigment content tracks the photosynthetic environment.

**DOC → Chl-a (β\* = 0.124, p = 0.229) — not significant**
Dissolved organic carbon from algae- or rubble-dominated communities did not detectably alter chlorophyll density. This suggests that heterotrophic carbon subsidies from the benthos either do not reach the symbiont compartment in quantities sufficient to alter pigment regulation, or that any effect is overwhelmed by the stronger pH signal. Organic carbon may be assimilated by the host without cascading into symbiont physiology.

**Chl-a → Endosymbiont density (β\* = 0.582, p < 0.001)**
Greater total chlorophyll per unit area is strongly associated with higher symbiont cell densities. This reflects the integral role of photosynthate in the coral–Symbiodiniaceae mutualism: symbionts that are photosynthetically active supply fixed carbon and energy to the host, which in turn maintains and expands the symbiont population. Chlorophyll density here acts as an indicator of the photosynthetic resource environment that enables higher cell retention.

**Endosymbiont density → Chl-a per cell (β\* = −0.275, p < 0.001)**
As symbiont cell density increases, chlorophyll content per cell declines — the classic **package effect**. When symbionts are densely packed within host tissue, cells mutually shade one another, reducing the light available to each cell. In response, individual cells downregulate pigment synthesis because additional chlorophyll provides diminishing photosynthetic returns under self-shading. This is a well-documented photoacclimation mechanism in Symbiodiniaceae and operates independently of the community-level chemistry signal.

**Endosymbiont density → Tissue biomass (β\* = 0.564, p < 0.001)**
Symbiont cell density is among the strongest predictors of host tissue biomass. Symbionts translocate 15–95% of photosynthetically fixed carbon to the host as glycerol, glucose, and lipids. A larger symbiont population delivers proportionally more photosynthate, fuelling lipid accumulation, protein synthesis, and tissue growth. This path represents the core nutritional benefit of the symbiosis.

**Endosymbiont density → Gross photosynthesis (β\* = 0.523, p < 0.001)**
More symbiont cells per unit area produce more photosynthetic oxygen because photosynthetic capacity is approximately additive across cells. This path captures the cellular-level scaling of reef metabolism: whole-colony oxygen production scales with the number of photosynthetically active cells housed in the tissue, making symbiont density the proximate driver of gross photosynthesis rates.

**Gross photosynthesis → Respiration (β\* = 0.540, p < 0.001)**
Photosynthesis and respiration are tightly coupled in coral tissue. Photosynthate generated by symbionts fuels aerobic metabolism in both the symbiont and host cell compartments, so higher gross photosynthesis directly drives higher oxygen consumption. This P:R coupling also reflects shared carbon allocation: carbohydrates fixed during the day are respired by mitochondria in both partners, producing a predictable positive relationship between the two fluxes.

**Tissue biomass → Respiration (β\* = 0.149, p = 0.110) — not significant**
Once gross photosynthesis is accounted for, standing tissue biomass does not significantly predict respiration. This suggests that metabolic rate is governed more by immediate energy flux (current photosynthate supply) than by the total quantity of tissue present. In corals, maintenance respiration per unit biomass appears relatively constant; the variation in respiration rate across fragments is better explained by the photosynthetic activity of their symbionts than by how much tissue has accumulated.

---

## R² for endogenous variables

```{r frag-path-r2}
#| label: frag-path-r2

singular_tbl <- tibble(
  Response = c("log_chla", "log_endo", "log_biomass", "mean_R", "mean_GP"),
  Singular = c(isSingular(frag_m_chla), isSingular(frag_m_endo),
               isSingular(frag_m_biomass), isSingular(frag_m_r),
               isSingular(frag_m_gp))
)

frag_r2_tbl <- rsquared(frag_psem) |>
  dplyr::select(Response, `R² / R²m` = Marginal, `R²c` = Conditional) |>
  left_join(singular_tbl, by = "Response") |>
  mutate(
    Response = recode(Response,
      log_chla    = "Chl-a (log)",
      log_endo    = "Endosymbiont (log)",
      log_biomass = "Tissue biomass (log)",
      mean_R      = "Respiration",
      mean_GP     = "Gross photosynthesis"),
    across(where(is.numeric), ~round(., 3))
  )

frag_r2_tbl |>
  kbl(
    caption = paste0(
      "R\u00b2 per path equation (piecewiseSEM). R\u00b2m (marginal) reflects ",
      "fixed-effect predictors only; R\u00b2c (conditional) includes fixed + ",
      "random effects (Nakagawa & Schielzeth 2013). ",
      "Singular = one random-effect variance component estimated at zero."
    ),
    col.names = c("Response", "R\u00b2 / R\u00b2m", "R\u00b2c", "Singular fit")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
```

---

## Random effects variance components

```{r frag-re-variances}
#| label: frag-re-variances

re_tbl <- purrr::map2_dfr(
  list(frag_m_chla, frag_m_endo, frag_m_gp, frag_m_r, frag_m_biomass),
  c("Chl-a (log)", "Endosymbiont (log)", "Gross photosynthesis",
    "Respiration", "Tissue biomass (log)"),
  function(mod, nm) {
    as.data.frame(VarCorr(mod)) |>
      dplyr::filter(is.na(var2)) |>
      dplyr::transmute(
        Response = nm,
        Group    = grp,
        Variance = round(vcov, 4),
        SD       = round(sdcor, 4)
      )
  }
)

re_tbl |>
  kbl(
    caption = "Random-effect variance components for each fragment-level equation. Variance \u2248 0 indicates singularity (that component estimated at its boundary).",
    col.names = c("Response", "Group", "Variance", "SD")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top")
```

---


# Supplementary figures {#sec-supp}

## Supp Fig 1 — Diel pH dynamics (24-hr experiment)

```{r load-24hr}
#| label: load-24hr

pHcalib_24 <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "TrisCalSummer2024.csv"),
                        show_col_types = FALSE)
pHData_24  <- read_csv(here("Data", "Chemistry", "24_hr_carb_chem.csv"),
                        show_col_types = FALSE)

pH_all_24 <- pHcalib_24 |>
  nest_by(TrisCalDate) |>
  mutate(fitpH = list(lm(mVTris ~ TTris, data = data))) |>
  reframe(broom::tidy(fitpH)) |>
  dplyr::select(TrisCalDate, term, estimate) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = term, values_from = estimate) |>
  right_join(pHData_24, by = "TrisCalDate") |>
  mutate(mVTris    = TEMPINLAB * TTris + `(Intercept)`) |>
  drop_na(TEMPINSITU, mV) |>
  mutate(
    pH_lab    = seacarb::pH(Ex = mV, Etris = mVTris, S = SALINITY, T = TEMPINLAB),
    pH_insitu = seacarb::pHinsi(pH = pH_lab, ALK = 2200, Tinsi = TEMPINSITU,
                                Tlab = TEMPINLAB, S = SALINITY, Pt = 0.1,
                                k1k2 = "m10", kf = "dg")
  )

InflowData_24 <- pH_all_24 |>
  filter(TANK_NUM %in% c("Inflow1", "Inflow2")) |>
  mutate(InflowTable = ifelse(TANK_NUM == "Inflow1", 1, 2)) |>
  dplyr::select(DATE, TIME, InflowTable, pH_inflow = pH_insitu)

Data_24 <- pH_all_24 |>
  filter(!TANK_NUM %in% c("Inflow1", "Inflow2")) |>
  mutate(TANK_NUM = as.numeric(TANK_NUM)) |>
  left_join(TableID, by = "TANK_NUM") |>
  left_join(InflowData_24, by = c("DATE", "TIME", "INFLOW_TABLE" = "InflowTable")) |>
  mutate(
    DATETIME  = ymd_hms(paste(as.character(DATE), TIME)),
    pHDiff    = pH_insitu - pH_inflow,
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels)
  )
```

```{r fig-supp1}
#| label: fig-supp1
#| fig-cap: "Change in pH relative to inflow across a diel cycle in the 24-hour mesocosm experiment. Each line represents one tank; points show individual measurement times. Yellow shading indicates daytime; grey shading indicates nighttime. The final morning measurement (Jun 03 06:00–09:00) occurred under overcast conditions."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5

fig_supp1 <- Data_24 |>
  group_by(DATETIME, TANK_NUM, TREATMENT) |>
  summarise(avg_pH_diff = mean(pHDiff, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = DATETIME, y = avg_pH_diff)) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = ymd_hms("2024-06-02 12:00:00"),
           xmax = ymd_hms("2024-06-02 18:00:00"),
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "#FFFACD", alpha = 0.6) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = ymd_hms("2024-06-02 18:00:00"),
           xmax = ymd_hms("2024-06-03 06:00:00"),
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "#D3D3D3", alpha = 0.4) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = ymd_hms("2024-06-03 06:00:00"),
           xmax = ymd_hms("2024-06-03 09:00:00"),
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = "#FFFACD", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2, color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_line(aes(group = TANK_NUM, color = TREATMENT), linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT), size = 2) +
  annotate("text", x = ymd_hms("2024-06-03 08:00:00"), y = 0.15,
           label = "Overcast\nDay", size = 3.2, color = "grey30") +
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
  scale_x_datetime(date_labels = "%b %d %H:%M", date_breaks = "6 hours") +
  labs(x = "Date & Time", y = "Change in pH") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "supp_fig1_diel_pH.png"),
       fig_supp1, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)

fig_supp1
```

---

## Supp Fig 2 — Coral physiology by treatment

```{r fig-supp2}
#| label: fig-supp2
#| fig-cap: "Coral fragment physiology by community treatment. Large points show treatment means; error bars are ±1 SD; small translucent points are individual fragments. Log₁₀ y-axis for Endosymbiont Density, Chlorophyll-a, and Tissue Biomass. No treatment effect was significant (mixed-effects models with genotype as random intercept, all p > 0.14)."
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 9

supp_panel_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

make_physio_panel <- function(yvar, ylab, log_y = FALSE) {
  p <- ggplot(
    metadata |> mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels)),
    aes(x = TREATMENT, y = .data[[yvar]], color = TREATMENT)
  ) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.25, size = 1.8,
               position = position_jitter(width = 0.08, seed = 42)) +
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3.5) +
    stat_summary(fun.data = mean_sdl, geom = "errorbar",
                 fun.args = list(mult = 1), width = 0.18, linewidth = 0.8) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = trt_labels) +
    scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = ylab) +
    supp_panel_theme
  if (log_y) p <- p + scale_y_log10()
  p
}

sp2a <- make_physio_panel("endo_per_cm2",
  expression(bold("Endosymbiont Density") ~ (cells %*% 10^6 ~ cm^{-2})), log_y = TRUE)
sp2b <- make_physio_panel("chla_ug_cm2",
  expression(bold("Chlorophyll-a Content") ~ (µg ~ cm^{-2})), log_y = TRUE)
sp2c <- make_physio_panel("mean_tissue_biomass",
  expression(bold("Tissue Biomass") ~ (mg ~ cm^{-2})), log_y = TRUE)
sp2d <- make_physio_panel("R",
  expression(bold("Respiration Rate") ~ (µmol ~ O[2] ~ cm^{-2} ~ hr^{-1})))
sp2e <- make_physio_panel("GP",
  expression(bold("Gross Photosynthesis") ~ (µmol ~ O[2] ~ cm^{-2} ~ hr^{-1})))

fig_supp2 <- (sp2a + sp2b + sp2c) / (sp2d + sp2e) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a")

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "supp_fig2_physiology_by_treatment.png"),
       fig_supp2, width = 11, height = 9, dpi = 300)

fig_supp2
```

---

## Supp Fig 3 — Physiology vs mean pH

```{r fig-supp3}
#| label: fig-supp3
#| fig-cap: "Coral physiology as a function of mean daytime pH (tank means, n = 16). Points colored by treatment. No relationships were significant at α = 0.05 (simple OLS; Chl-a: p = 0.09, Endosymbiont Density: p = 0.84, Tissue Biomass: p = 0.67, Respiration Rate: p = 0.06, Gross Photosynthesis: p = 0.50). Y-axes for Chl-a, Endosymbiont Density, and Tissue Biomass are on a log₁₀ scale."
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 9

supp_scatter_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

raw_frag_path <- metadata |>
  mutate(mean_tissue_biomass = ifelse(mean_tissue_biomass > 30, NA, mean_tissue_biomass)) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarize(nep = mean(NEP, na.rm = TRUE),
                ph  = max(pH,   na.rm = TRUE),
                doc = mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
                .groups = "drop"),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

tank_phys_chem <- raw_frag_path |>
  group_by(TANK_NUM, TREATMENT) |>
  summarise(
    mean_chla    = mean(chla_ug_cm2,        na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_endos   = mean(endo_per_cm2,       na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_biomass = mean(mean_tissue_biomass, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_R       = mean(R,                  na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_GP      = mean(GP,                 na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  left_join(
    chem_reframe_DAY_clean |>
      group_by(TANK_NUM) |>
      summarise(grand_mean_pH  = mean(pH,  na.rm = TRUE),
                grand_mean_DOC = mean(DOC, na.rm = TRUE),
                .groups = "drop"),
    by = "TANK_NUM"
  ) |>
  mutate(TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = treatment_levels))

make_scatter <- function(xvar, yvar, xlab, ylab, add_lm = FALSE, log_y = FALSE) {
  p <- ggplot(tank_phys_chem,
              aes(x = .data[[xvar]], y = .data[[yvar]], color = TREATMENT)) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5) +
    scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL) +
    labs(x = xlab, y = ylab) +
    supp_scatter_theme
  if (add_lm) p <- p + geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x,
                                    color = "grey30", se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.8)
  if (log_y)  p <- p + scale_y_log10()
  p
}

ph_xlab  <- expression("pH"[T])
chla_ylab  <- expression("Chlorophyll-a Content (" * mu * "g cm"^{-2} * ")")
endo_ylab  <- expression("Endosymbiont Density (cells " * 10^6 ~ "cm"^{-2} * ")")
bio_ylab   <- expression("Mean Tissue Biomass (mg cm"^{-2} * ")")
R_ylab     <- expression("Respiration Rate (" * mu * "mol O"[2] * " cm"^{-2} * " hr"^{-1} * ")")
GP_ylab    <- expression("Gross Photosynthesis (" * mu * "mol O"[2] * " cm"^{-2} * " hr"^{-1} * ")")

sp3a <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_chla",    ph_xlab, chla_ylab, log_y = TRUE)
sp3b <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_endos",   ph_xlab, endo_ylab, log_y = TRUE)
sp3c <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_biomass", ph_xlab, bio_ylab,  log_y = TRUE)
sp3d <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_R",       ph_xlab, R_ylab)
sp3e <- make_scatter("grand_mean_pH", "mean_GP",      ph_xlab, GP_ylab)

fig_supp3 <- (sp3a + sp3b + sp3c) / (sp3d + sp3e) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a") +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") &
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL)

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "supp_fig3_physiology_vs_pH.png"),
       fig_supp3, width = 12, height = 9, dpi = 300)

fig_supp3
```

---

## Supp Fig 4 — Physiology vs mean DOC

```{r fig-supp4}
#| label: fig-supp4
#| fig-cap: "Coral physiology as a function of mean daytime DOC (tank means, n = 16). Points colored by treatment. No significant linear relationships were detected. Y-axes for Chl-a, Endosymbiont Density, and Tissue Biomass are on a log₁₀ scale."
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 9

doc_xlab <- expression("DOC (" * mu * "mol L"^{-1} * ")")

sp4a <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_chla",    doc_xlab, chla_ylab, log_y = TRUE)
sp4b <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_endos",   doc_xlab, endo_ylab, log_y = TRUE)
sp4c <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_biomass", doc_xlab, bio_ylab,  log_y = TRUE)
sp4d <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_R",       doc_xlab, R_ylab)
sp4e <- make_scatter("grand_mean_DOC", "mean_GP",      doc_xlab, GP_ylab)

fig_supp4 <- (sp4a + sp4b + sp4c) / (sp4d + sp4e) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "a") +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") &
  scale_color_manual(values = trt_colors, labels = trt_labels, name = NULL)

ggsave(here("Revision", "Output", "supp_fig4_physiology_vs_DOC.png"),
       fig_supp4, width = 12, height = 9, dpi = 300)

fig_supp4
```
